{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01Jzhk8VSi94ZWqLS8Gv3p4Q","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The user wants me to provide a clinical pharmacogenomics recommendation for netilmicin (an aminoglycoside antibiotic) in a patient with MT-RNR1 uncertain risk of aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss genotype.\n\nLet me think about this:\n\n**MT-RNR1 and Aminoglycoside-Induced Hearing Loss:**\n- MT-RNR1 encodes the 12S ribosomal RNA in mitochondria\n- Certain variants in MT-RNR1, particularly m.1555A>G and m.1494C>T, are well-known to cause hypersensitivity to aminoglycosides, leading to irreversible sensorineural hearing loss\n\n\n- The m.1555A>G variant is especially problematic because it alters the mitochondrial ribosome to resemble bacterial ribosomes, making it susceptible to aminoglycoside binding and cochlear damage, with even single doses potentially causing permanent deafness in carriers\n- Other MT-RNR1 variants fall into an uncertain category where the ototoxic risk from aminoglycosides remains unclear\n\nFor uncertain risk variants, the clinical approach is more cautious—while the risk isn't as established as with m.1555A>G, guidelines like CPIC recommend preferring alternative antibiotics when possible, though aminoglycosides can be used if necessary (such as in life-threatening infections) with careful audiologic monitoring. Now I'm structuring the clinical guidance into a JSON response that consolidates the recommendation, risk classification, and mechanism of action for this variant.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1186,"cache_read_input_tokens":18394,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1186,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"460689c6-2b46-4cea-8585-fb80915bcd2b","uuid":"b64d8ced-c598-460a-9dd9-f6b2d76c1aed"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01Jzhk8VSi94ZWqLS8Gv3p4Q","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01NBrjWcbe3ry2X9omYvuiY7","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat > /app/answers.json <<'JSON'\n{\n  \"recommendation\": \"Use with caution. Consider alternative non-aminoglycoside antibiotics when clinically appropriate options exist. If netilmicin is required (e.g., no suitable alternatives for a serious infection), use the lowest effective dose for the shortest necessary duration. Audiologic monitoring is recommended where feasible, and patients should be counseled about the potential risk of irreversible sensorineural hearing loss.\",\n  \"classification\": \"Moderate\",\n  \"implication\": \"MT-RNR1 uncertain risk variants may potentially increase susceptibility to aminoglycoside-induced sensorineural hearing loss, though evidence supporting pathogenicity is less established than for high-risk variants (e.g., m.1555A>G, m.1494C>T). {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01A7WdBwa4NUnRAeVLaj3Rwf","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The file has been written and verified. Here is a summary of the clinical reasoning:\n\n**MT-RNR1 & Aminoglycoside Ototoxicity Background**\n\nMT-RNR1 encodes the mitochondrial 12S rRNA. High-risk variants (m.1555A>G, m.1494C>T) are well-established causes of aminoglycoside-induced sensorineural hearing loss — even a single dose can cause permanent deafness in carriers. These variants structurally alter the mitochondrial ribosome to resemble bacterial ribosomes, making it a target for aminoglycosides like netilmicin.\n\n**Uncertain Risk Category**\n\nFor variants of uncertain risk, the pathogenic evidence is incomplete or conflicting. The variant may or may not confer meaningful cochlear susceptibility. Because:\n- The potential harm (irreversible hearing loss) is severe and irreversible\n- Alternative antibiotics may be available\n- The uncertainty itself warrants precaution\n\n...the recommendation is **Moderate** strength caution rather than a contraindication.\n\n**Key clinical actions:**\n- Prefer non-aminoglycoside alternatives when clinically equivalent\n- If netilmicin is necessary: lowest dose, shortest duration, audiologic monitoring\n- Patient counseling on ototoxicity risk"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":608,"cache_read_input_tokens":21338,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":608,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"460689c6-2b46-4cea-8585-fb80915bcd2b","uuid":"bc1919ac-3011-4ac7-94c6-a2849de94716"}
